title: Intersection of Two Lists
description:
Given heads of two singly linked lists, return the node where the two lists intersect, or None if they do not intersect. Intersection is by reference, not value.

    

Constraints:
    - 0 ≤ n,m ≤ 10^5
explanation:
Intuition
Traverse A then B with one pointer, and B then A with the other. If the lists intersect, the pointers will meet at the intersection node after equalizing path lengths without extra memory.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Initialize p=headA, q=headB.
2. Main Algorithm: Advance each; when a pointer reaches None, redirect it to the head of the other list.
3. Return Result: Stop when p is q and return that node (possibly None).

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n+m) due to a single pass or divide-and-conquer
  over the list where each node is visited a bounded number of times.
- Space Complexity: O(1) accounting for pointers and, when needed,
  recursion depth or extra structures.

Key Insights
- Switching heads synchronizes remaining distances
- Works without computing lengths

Common Pitfalls
- Comparing by value instead of reference is incorrect
- Forgetting to allow None equality at the end
hints: Traverse both lists, switching heads at the end
Meet at intersection or at None
